sanskrit:
यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः॥
transliteration:
yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate ca yaḥ |
hṛṣāmārṣa-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa ca me priyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: From whom people are never agitated, and who is never agitated by people; who is freed from excitement, resentment, fear and anxiety—he too is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the psychologically non-reactive devotee: an individual whose inner equilibrium is so stable that neither their presence provokes distress in others, nor can others disturb their equanimity. This is the mature attachment style of ‘secure base’—non-intrusive, non-retaliatory, emotionally self-regulated.
psychological_state: Secure/autonomic coherence: low amygdala reactivity, high vagal tone; inner safe haven that radiates interpersonal safety.
trigger_scenario: A team-lead stays unruffled when a colleague snaps during a deadline crisis, neither snapping back nor avoiding; the room soon calms and productivity resumes.
anchor_text: Stay calm so others feel safe and nothing shakes you.
